key_insight: A hierarchical stability-screening pipeline can turn many computationally stable compounds into a much smaller set of experimentally plausible synthesis targets.
engineering_relevance: The framework helps materials engineers prioritize candidates using progressively stricter thermodynamic and dynamical filters before committing to costly experimental synthesis.
method: Curated 603 unique structures from 894 previously reported stable materials.
Applied complete PBE phase diagrams, then harmonic-phonon and finite-temperature MD dynamical screening.
Refined with SCAN phase diagrams and ranked candidates by decomposition enthalpy plus chemical-space completeness.
evidence_signal: From the 603 curated structures, only 298 remained PBE-stable, 166 passed both dynamical criteria, 109 survived SCAN refinement, and 25 were prioritized as high-confidence synthesis targets.
watch_item: The screening depends on the quality and completeness of competing-phase databases and model-based dynamical stability predictions, so some true experimental targets may still be missed.
paper_title: Predicting Novel Stable Materials for Experimental Synthesis
